creator: zyj20@cl.cam.ac.uk
text:
Created attachment 256238
repro.c

- Overview:
In ng_unparse_composite, a carefully crafted Netgraph generic message can cause OOB read of several bytes depending on the declared Netgraph composite type. This is not critical because, as far as I know, only privileged users can send Netgraph generic messages.

- Root cause:
In ng_unparse_composite, the offset off can be incremented to overflow the allocated length of the provided data buffer. When processing the NGM_BINARY2ASCII control message, the netgraph message is verified to be valid. However, if the indicated Netgraph composite type is of greater size than the provided data buffer, ng_unparse_composite will unparse out-of-bounds data in https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-src/blob/225c5e535b36ab506f25761fabc7dbe466609541/sys/netgraph/ng_parse.c#L1414 . 
This crash was found when working with a CheriBSD purecap kernel, where out-of-bounds read causes a kernel panic.

- Steps to reproduce:
Please see attached source code for proof of concept.
$ cc -o ng_parse_poc repro.c -lnetgraph -lpthread

- Kernel commit and config:
This is reproducible as of the latest commit on 27th December 2024 in a GENERIC kernel, with enabled Netgraph.

- Credits:
Yichen Chai <yichen.chai@gmail.com>
Zhuo Ying Jiang Li <zyj20@cl.cam.ac.uk>
